description: An employee asks for recognition after a strong quarter.

background_log_entries.note: Asked to discuss role growth after leading the Atlas release.
Budget review noted two promotion slots still unallocated.
Stayed late to unblock a customer escalation without being asked.

activity_signals.signal: 4 features shipped this quarter, including the Atlas release
Mentored two new engineers through onboarding
Has not held a growth check-in with Rohan in 5 months

pre_conversation_briefing.participant_a_sees: You have specific evidence of expanded impact. Ask for a concrete timeline and criteria, rather than only a yes or no today.
pre_conversation_briefing.participant_b_sees: Rohan's scope has grown measurably. If budget is constrained, avoid a dead end: offer transparent criteria and a dated next review.

scripted_conversation.speaker: Rohan
scripted_conversation.message: Thanks for making time. I wanted to talk about my compensation and growth path.

scripted_conversation.speaker: Priya
scripted_conversation.message: Of course. What feels most important to discuss?

scripted_conversation.speaker: Rohan
scripted_conversation.message: I led Atlas, shipped four features this quarter, and have been mentoring the new hires. I think my salary should reflect that.

scripted_conversation.speaker: Priya
scripted_conversation.message: I see the impact. The budget is tight right now, so I can't promise a raise today.

scripted_conversation.speaker: Rohan
scripted_conversation.message: I understand, but I don't want this to become an open-ended maybe.

scripted_conversation.speaker: Priya
scripted_conversation.message: That's fair. Let's review the promotion criteria together this week and set a compensation decision for the July 15 cycle.

follow_up.commitment: Priya will review Rohan's promotion criteria this week and make a compensation decision by July 15.
follow_up.success_note: The review happened on schedule, and the decision was communicated clearly.
follow_up.stalled_note: The July 15 decision date has passed. Could you both schedule a 20-minute follow-up to close the loop?
